platform_tradeoffs: Six medium-confidence terminal_log snapshots on an 8 GB system show compressed_memory_gb consistently at 2.33–2.40 GB (approximately 70% of app_memory_gb of 3.34–3.41 GB), indicating active memory compression even during idle and moderate workloads; this is a direct numeric signal that the current 8 GB pool is too small for the observed process set.

CPU load_pattern reports as 'idle' with cpu_cores_active of 0.85–1.07 across all recorded scenarios, including heavy_multitask and antigravity_active; this is consistent with CPUs stalling on memory compression and decompression overhead rather than being compute-limited, and suggests effective CPU throughput will improve once RAM pressure is resolved.

Seven medium-confidence idle_browser macos_unified_log records capture WebKit.GPU Critical memory pressure events across four distinct timestamps on 2026-06-28 (06:09, 08:35, 10:05, 14:59); four additional heavy_multitask records on 2026-06-28 to 2026-06-29 show system-wide Critical pressure from Google Drive, WebKit.GPU, and Finder without any local LLM inference running, confirming browser and sync-driven GPU memory contention is a baseline condition.

Jetsam_Kills_365d.log and RAM_Crashes_365d.log are both empty over the past year; no hard process terminations or RAM crashes have occurred, indicating the 8 GB system manages pressure through compression and minor swap activity rather than catastrophic failure — but this does not indicate RAM is sufficient, only that degradation is tolerated rather than causing visible failures.

A 32 GB system memory floor is provisionally supported by the observed compression burden, recurring critical pressure events, and the concurrent process set (Antigravity IDE, multiple Comet agents, Claude, Google Drive, WebKit.GPU); at 32 GB the active workloads should fit without compression-induced stalls in most scenarios, though relief cycles may still occur during peak background sync activity.

A 64 GB recommended target is unchanged: it provides headroom to isolate foreground workloads (IDE, agents, browser) from persistent background services (Google Drive, WebKit.GPU, QuickLook), eliminating the relief-cycle overhead visible in current logs and leaving room for heavier local models or larger context windows in future workloads.

Apple unified memory at 64 GB covers both CPU and GPU memory demands from a single pool, removing WebKit.GPU contention as a discrete bottleneck; 32 GB is a strict floor but may still incur GPU-side pressure relief cycles during concurrent browser and IDE use as observed in current logs.

Discrete GPU platforms (AMD, NVIDIA) provisionally require a minimum of 12 GB VRAM (recommended 16 GB) to reduce WebKit.GPU exhaustion risk during multitasking; system RAM should be planned independently at 32 GB or higher to avoid CPU-side bottlenecks from background process contention.
Gemma 9B telemetry run: 8 GB system hitting 35MB free RAM and heavy compression is "confirmed insufficient" for 9B-class local inference.
confidence_notes: Second-pass analysis over the same 23-record corpus finds no new telemetry evidence to move any numeric floor, recommended, or stretch value. RAM dimension remains the best-supported: six medium-confidence terminal_log records consistently show a ~70% compression ratio on 8 GB across idle and active scenarios; the macos_unified_log series (records 12–23) adds seven idle_browser Critical WebKit.GPU pressure events and four heavy_multitask Critical pressure events (Google Drive + WebKit.GPU + Finder combos), all without local LLM inference, independently corroborating the Browser_GPU_Memory_365d.log year-wide pattern. The empty Jetsam_Kills_365d.log and RAM_Crashes_365d.log remain meaningful: no hard process kills have occurred over the past year, indicating tolerance of degradation through compression rather than catastrophic failure, but this does not weaken the 32 GB floor. The sample_telemetry.log record (16 GB, Normal, Chrome + WindowServer) confirms 16 GB is workable for light browser use but provides no IDE or agent scenario signal. CPU dimension is weakly supported: all records with cpu_telemetry report load_pattern 'idle' and cpu_cores_active 0.85–1.07, most likely reflecting memory-compression stall rather than true CPU idle; core_count_min of 4 remains a planning assumption. GPU and local inference dimensions remain unsupported by direct measurement: no gpu_telemetry or inference_telemetry is present in any record; VRAM targets are grounded in WebKit.GPU pressure event frequency only. The single most useful follow-up measurement would be a vm_stat or memory_pressure capture with swap visibility enabled during a heavy_multitask session — specifically capturing swap_used_gb and compressed_memory_gb simultaneously — to quantify swap magnitude and confirm whether it correlates with the CPU stalling pattern observed in terminal_log records.

cpu_tier.rationale: All medium-confidence records with cpu_telemetry report load_pattern 'idle' and cpu_cores_active of 0.85–1.07 even under antigravity_active and heavy_multitask scenario labels, consistent with memory-compression stall suppressing apparent CPU utilization. The concurrent process set (WindowServer, Antigravity IDE, multiple Comet agents, claude.exe, Google Drive) and the burst pattern of IDE and agent activity support single_thread_priority and planning for sustained_load once RAM bottlenecking is resolved; core_count_min of 4 is a conservative planning floor in the absence of unthrottled CPU telemetry.

platform_classes.caveats: Metal-only inference; unified memory serves both CPU and GPU from a single pool. A 64 GB target is provisionally recommended to isolate IDE, agents, and browser from persistent background Google Drive and WebKit.GPU memory demands; 32 GB serves as a strict floor but may still incur GPU-side pressure relief cycles during concurrent browser and multitask workloads as documented in current logs.

ROCm support is model/driver dependent; verify compatibility before purchase. RDNA2 (RX 6800M) is DEFER — persistent kernel panics during autoregressive generation and absent ExLlamaV2 support. RDNA3 (RX 7900M) has partial support; evaluate at same risk threshold as NVIDIA. Discrete VRAM is a hard ceiling — provisionally size to 16 GB (12 GB minimum). System RAM must be planned independently at 32 GB or higher.

platform_classes.architecture_minimum_reason: Ampere is the first generation with native Flash Attention support. Turing (SM75) — Quadro RTX 5000/6000 — lacks hardware-accelerated Flash Attention 2; fallback kernels incur severe latency on context windows above 4K. Any pre-Ampere NVIDIA GPU is DEFER regardless of VRAM capacity.
platform_classes.caveats: CUDA ecosystem; discrete VRAM is a hard ceiling — size to 16 GB (12 GB minimum). 16 GB target supports 13B Q4 models at 25+ tok/s sustained with 4 GB KV-cache headroom. System RAM must be planned independently at 32 GB or higher to avoid CPU-side bottlenecks from background process contention.
